Package io.forja.builder

Class FxNodeBuilder<T extends javafx.scene.Node,B extends FxNodeBuilder<T,B>>

java.lang.Object
io.forja.builder.FxNodeBuilder<T,B>
Type Parameters:
T - the type of component this builder produces
B - the concrete builder subclass type
Direct Known Subclasses:
FxAccordion.Builder, FxAlert.Builder, FxAnchorNav.Builder, FxAppBar.Builder, FxAreaChart.Builder, FxAspectRatio.Builder, FxAudioPlayer.Builder, FxAutocomplete.Builder, FxAvatar.Builder, FxAvatarGroup.Builder, FxBanner.Builder, FxBarChart.Builder, FxBlockquote.Builder, FxBreadcrumbs.Builder, FxBulletList.Builder, FxButtonGroup.Builder, FxCalendar.Builder, FxCard.Builder, FxCarousel.Builder, FxCheckGroup.Builder, FxCodeEditor.Builder, FxCollapse.Builder, FxCommandPalette.Builder, FxContainer.Builder, FxDataGrid.Builder, FxDatePicker.Builder, FxDateRangePicker.Builder, FxImageGallery.Builder, FxImagePicker.Builder, FxJsonEditor.Builder, FxKanbanBoard.Builder, FxKeybindingHint.Builder, FxLineChart.Builder, FxMarkdownEditor.Builder, FxMaskedInput.Builder, FxMasonry.Builder, FxMiniCalendar.Builder, FxMultiSelect.Builder, FxNumberField.Builder, FxOTPInput.Builder, FxPagination.Builder, FxPasswordField.Builder, FxPieChart.Builder, FxRadarChart.Builder, FxRadioGroup.Builder, FxRangeSlider.Builder, FxRating.Builder, FxResizablePane.Builder, FxResponsive.Builder, FxResultPage.Builder, FxRichTextEditor.Builder, FxRow.Builder, FxScatterPlot.Builder, FxSearchField.Builder, FxSearchHighlight.Builder, FxSection.Builder, FxSidebar.Builder, FxSidebarNav.Builder, FxSkeleton.Builder, FxSlider.Builder, FxSpacer.Builder, FxSparkline.Builder, FxStack.Builder, FxStat.Builder, FxStatusDot.Builder, FxStepper.Builder, FxStickyHeader.Builder, FxSwitch.Builder, FxTagInput.Builder, FxTextArea.Builder, FxTextField.Builder, FxTimeline.Builder, FxTimePicker.Builder, FxToast.Builder, FxVideoPlayer.Builder, FxVirtualList.Builder, FxWaveform.Builder
public abstract class FxNodeBuilder<T extends javafx.scene.Node,B extends FxNodeBuilder<T,B>> extends Object
Abstract base builder for Forja component builders whose target is a Node but not a Control.

Mirrors FxComponentBuilder but with a relaxed bound. Use this for components such as FxIcon, FxStatusDot, FxSpacer, and the layout primitives (FxStack, FxRow, FxContainer, FxSection) which extend Node subclasses (Shape, Region) that are not Controls.

The base exposes the shared property surface common to every Forja non-Control component — id, visibility, CSS style classes, and user data — but omits the Control-only properties (disabled, tooltip, inline FxStyle overrides) that FxComponentBuilder provides.

The double generic pattern ensures fluent chaining works correctly across the inheritance hierarchy. T is the component type being built, B is the concrete builder subclass: 


 public static class Builder extends FxNodeBuilder<FxIcon, Builder> {
     ...
 }

Every subclass must override build() and call applyBase(Node) on the constructed node before returning it: 


 public FxIcon build() {
     FxIcon icon = new FxIcon(literal);
     applyBase(icon);
     return icon;
 }

  • Constructor Details

    • FxNodeBuilder

      public FxNodeBuilder()

  • Method Details

    • id

      public B id(String id)
      Sets the CSS id of the component.
      Parameters:
      id - the CSS id, must not be null
      Returns:
      this builder

    • visible

      public B visible(boolean visible)
      Sets the visibility of the component. Defaults to true.
      Parameters:
      visible - false to hide the component
      Returns:
      this builder

    • styleClass

      public B styleClass(String... classes)
      Adds one or more CSS style classes to the component.

      Classes are appended to the component's existing style classes — they never replace the component's own Forja style class.

      Parameters:
      classes - one or more CSS class names to add
      Returns:
      this builder

    • userData

      public B userData(Object userData)
      Attaches arbitrary user data to the component.

      Accessible later via Node.getUserData().

      Parameters:
      userData - the object to attach, or null
      Returns:
      this builder

    • applyBase

      protected void applyBase(T node)
      Applies all shared base properties to the given node.

      Must be called by every subclass build() implementation before returning the constructed node. Properties are applied in the following order:

      1. CSS id
      2. Visibility
      3. Style classes
      4. User data
      Parameters:
      node - the node to configure, must not be null

    • build

      public abstract T build()
      Constructs and returns the configured component.

      Implementations must call applyBase(Node) on the constructed node before returning it.

      Returns:
      the built component
